purpose: add_rules_from_file's malformed arms via the apply_input raising probe: a missing file raises the bare open() FileNotFoundError (errno message embedding the repo-resolved path — identical for both engines run from this repo); the bad middle line of rules-badline.txt raises ValueError "Line 2: Failed to parse rule ..." under raise_errors=True AFTER loading line 1 (the partial load pinned by the following fingerprint: [rule_0]); the same file under the default raise_errors=False warns, skips the bad line, and leaves the name gap rule_1/rule_3 (i indexes the filtered line list even for failed lines, pyreason.py:677) — the malformed-missing-file, malformed-line-raise, and malformed-line-skip classes plus the raise-then-continue state contamination.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_rules_from_file malformed-* classes; pyreason.py:669-690 at the pin; exception text screened live 2026-07-07; fixture harness/fixtures/rules-badline.txt
